esercizi.nome: Hands-on-Shoulders Elbow Circles
esercizi.description: This movement improves shoulder mobility and helps reduce stiffness when raising the arms, dressing, or washing the upper body.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright or stand. Place your right hand on your right shoulder and your left hand on your left shoulder. Slowly draw small circles with your elbows, moving them forward, upward, backward, and down. Make 5 circles forward and then 5 circles backward.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move slowly and keep your neck relaxed.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make large circles if they cause shoulder pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Hands-on-Shoulders Elbow Circles

esercizi.nome: Bring the Hands Behind the Neck
esercizi.description: This exercise helps stretch and open the front part of the shoulder. It reproduces the movement used to comb your hair, wash your hair, or put on a shirt over your head.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair or stand. Lift your arms out to the sides and bend your elbows, bringing the palms of your hands to gently touch the back of your head, the nape of your neck. From this position, push your elbows backward as far as is comfortable, feeling your chest open. Hold this position for 5 seconds, then relax your arms by your sides.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your neck relaxed and always look straight ahead.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not push your head forward with your hands and do not round your back.
esercizi.imageAlt: Bring the Hands Behind the Neck

esercizi.nome: Press the Palms Together
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the anterior muscles of the shoulder and chest. It is very useful for maintaining the strength needed to hold an object close to your body, such as a shopping bag or a tray.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright. Bring the palms of your hands together in front of your chest, keeping your elbows raised at the same height as your hands, as in a prayer position. Gently push one hand against the other, as if you were trying to squeeze something between your palms. Maintain a steady push for 5 seconds, then relax.
esercizi.whatToDo: Continue breathing normally throughout the push.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not raise your shoulders toward your ears and do not clench your teeth.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Palms Together

esercizi.nome: Raise the Arms Forward
esercizi.description: This movement strengthens the front part of the shoulder. It simulates the action of placing a plate on a shelf or passing an object to someone in front of you.
esercizi.howToDo: You can sit or stand. Keep your arms by your sides with your palms facing your thighs. Slowly lift your straight arms forward in front of you until they reach eye level. If you want, you can hold two small water bottles, either empty or half full. Hold your arms up for 3 seconds, then slowly lower them back down.
esercizi.whatToDo: Perform the movement very slowly, both when lifting and lowering your arms.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not swing your torso backward to help lift your arms.
esercizi.imageAlt: Raise the Arms Forward

esercizi.nome: Finger Walk on the Wall
esercizi.description: This exercise combines stretching with strengthening of the front part of the shoulder, helping you recover upward arm movement without putting too much strain on your back.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand facing a wall, about one step away. Place your fingertips on the wall at chest height. Using your fingers as if they were the legs of a spider, make them “walk” up the wall, one small step at a time, as far as you can comfortably reach. Stay at the highest point for 5 seconds, then make your fingers “walk” back down.
esercizi.whatToDo: Take a small step closer to the wall as your hand moves upward, so you do not strain your back.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your heels off the floor and do not force the upward movement if you feel shoulder pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Finger Walk on the Wall

esercizi.nome: Seated Shoulder Blade Push Forward
esercizi.description: This exercise activates the muscles that help the shoulder blade move forward, which is useful when reaching for an object on a table or pushing something light away.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair with your feet resting on the floor. Stretch both arms forward at chest height, with your hands open and your palms facing each other. Without bending your elbows, gently push your hands forward as if you wanted to reach something a little farther away. You should feel your shoulder blades move slightly apart. Hold for 3 seconds, then return to the starting position.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your arms straight and move slowly from the shoulders.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not round your whole back forward and do not lift your shoulders toward your ears.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Shoulder Blade Push Forward

esercizi.nome: Quadruped Single Arm Reach
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the shoulder and improves trunk stability while the arm moves forward. It is useful for reaching for objects safely.
esercizi.howToDo: Get on all fours on a mat. Keep your hands under your shoulders and your knees under your hips. Slowly lift your right arm forward, keeping it straight, until it reaches the height of your shoulder. Hold for 2 seconds, then place the hand back down. Repeat 5 times, then switch arms.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your abdomen slightly engaged to prevent your body from swaying.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not rotate your torso and do not lift the arm higher than shoulder level.
esercizi.imageAlt: Quadruped Single Arm Reach

esercizi.nome: Raise the Arms to the Side
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the side part of the shoulder. It reproduces the movement used to put on the sleeves of a coat or jacket, or to open your arms out to take measurements.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair or stand. Keep your arms relaxed by your sides, with your palms facing inward. Slowly lift your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, as if you had wings. Hold the position for 3 seconds, then lower your arms very slowly.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your palms facing the floor as you lift your arms.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not raise your hands above shoulder height and do not make quick, sudden movements.
esercizi.imageAlt: Raise the Arms to the Side

esercizi.nome: Side Elbow Press Against the Wall
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the side muscles without moving the joint, making it excellent for people with mild pain. It simulates the strength needed to move an object sideways with your body or to make space for yourself.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand sideways next to a wall, about half an arm’s length away. Bend your elbow to 90 degrees and place the outer side of your arm and elbow against the wall. Push your elbow sideways against the wall, as if you were trying to knock it down. Maintain a steady push for 5 seconds, then relax and switch sides.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your body straight and still; only the shoulder should push.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lean your torso toward the wall to help yourself and do not clench your teeth.
esercizi.imageAlt: Side Elbow Press Against the Wall

esercizi.nome: Open the Arms as if Cleaning the Table
esercizi.description: This exercise combines side shoulder movement with an action you do every day at home, such as cleaning furniture or spreading out a tablecloth.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright in front of a table. Place the palms of your hands on the table in front of you, with your elbows slightly bent. Without lifting your hands from the surface, slide both hands outward at the same time, opening them as far as possible. Then, still sliding, bring them back to the centre. Repeat this movement 5 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: You can place two soft cloths under your hands to help them slide more easily.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your hands off the table and do not bend your back forward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Open the Arms as if Cleaning the Table

esercizi.nome: Sideways Finger Walk on the Wall
esercizi.description: This exercise helps improve the side opening of the arms and mobility, which is essential for reaching an object placed beside you without having to turn your whole body.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand sideways next to a wall, with your right side facing the wall. Place the fingertips of your right hand on the wall at shoulder height. Using your fingers, make them “walk” up the wall, moving your arm out to the side as far as you can comfortably reach. Stay there for 5 seconds, then slowly come back down and repeat with your left side facing the wall.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your body fully turned sideways, without rotating your chest toward the wall.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not force the movement if you feel a sharp pain or strong pain in your shoulder.
esercizi.imageAlt: Sideways Finger Walk on the Wall

esercizi.nome: Push the Elbows Backward
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the back part of the shoulder and the back. It reproduces the movement used to put the second arm into the sleeve of a coat or to tie an apron behind your waist.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair, away from the backrest. Raise your elbows to chest height and bend your arms, keeping your hands close to your chest. From this position, gently push your elbows backward, as if you were giving a light elbow push to a wall behind you. Count to 5, then return forward.
esercizi.whatToDo: Bring the two flat bones in your upper back, the shoulder blades, closer together as you push backward.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not raise your shoulders toward your ears and do not tilt your head forward.
esercizi.imageAlt: Push the Elbows Backward

esercizi.nome: Backward Elbow Press Against the Backrest
esercizi.description: This exercise safely strengthens the posterior muscles using the chair, helping improve posture when sitting at a table or in an armchair.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright and rest your back against the backrest of the chair. Keep your arms by your sides and bend your elbows to 90 degrees, keeping them close to your body. Push your elbows firmly backward against the backrest of the chair. Maintain this steady push for 5 seconds, then relax.
esercizi.whatToDo: Continue breathing normally and keep your feet firmly planted on the floor.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your back away from the chair and do not make sudden movements.
esercizi.imageAlt: Backward Elbow Press Against the Backrest

esercizi.nome: Bring the Hands Behind the Back
esercizi.description: This movement stretches the front part and activates the muscles at the back, simulating the action of putting a belt through your trousers, washing your lower back, or scratching your back.
esercizi.howToDo: You can sit or stand. Bring your arms behind your back at waist level. Try to hold your left wrist with your right hand, or vice versa. If you cannot do this, simply place the backs of your hands on your lower back. From this position, gently push your shoulders backward. Hold this position for 5 seconds.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your chest open and look straight ahead.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not bend your neck forward and do not force the movement if you feel shoulder pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Bring the Hands Behind the Back

esercizi.nome: Open the Straight Arms Backward
esercizi.description: This exercise helps give elasticity and strength to the entire posterior chain of the shoulder, which is essential for preventing a forward-rounded posture when walking.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on the chair. Bring your straight arms forward at chest height, with your palms facing each other. From this position, open your arms out to the sides and bring them as far back as comfortably possible, as if you were trying to give someone a very big hug. Hold the maximum opening position for 3 seconds, then return forward.
esercizi.whatToDo: Perform the movement very slowly, controlling the return.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not arch your lower back and do not hold your breath.
esercizi.imageAlt: Open the Straight Arms Backward

esercizi.nome: Shoulder External Rotation with Elbows Close
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the small stabilizing muscles of the shoulder, which help keep the arm steady during daily activities such as dressing or reaching for objects.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Bend both elbows to 90 degrees and keep them close to your sides. Keep your palms facing upward. Slowly move your hands outward, opening your forearms to the sides while keeping your elbows attached to your body. Hold for 2 seconds, then return to the starting position. Repeat 5 times.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your elbows close to your sides throughout the movement.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your shoulders and do not rotate your torso to help the movement.
esercizi.imageAlt: Shoulder External Rotation with Elbows Close

esercizi.nome: Seated Shoulder Blade Squeeze Downward
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the muscles that keep the shoulders low and stable, helping reduce tension in the neck and upper back.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair with your arms relaxed by your sides. Imagine that you want to slide your shoulder blades down toward your back pockets. Gently pull your shoulders downward and slightly backward, without moving your head. Hold the position for 5 seconds, then relax completely.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your neck long and your gaze straight ahead.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not push your chest too far forward and do not hold your breath.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Shoulder Blade Squeeze Downward

esercizi.nome: Towel Pull Apart
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the back part of the shoulders and upper back, helping improve posture and arm control.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright or stand. Hold a small towel with both hands in front of your chest, keeping your elbows slightly bent. Pull the towel outward with both hands, as if you were trying to stretch it apart. Keep the pull steady for 5 seconds, then relax.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your wrists straight and your shoulders relaxed.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not pull so hard that your neck becomes tense.
esercizi.imageAlt: Towel Pull Apart

esercizi.nome: Touch the Opposite Shoulder
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the muscles at the front of the arm and improves mobility. It reproduces the movement used to wash yourself in the shower or to put on a scarf.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Keep your arms relaxed by your sides. Slowly lift your right hand and bring it to rest on your left shoulder, bending your elbow in front of your chest. Hold still for 3 seconds, then slowly lower your arm back down. Repeat the same movement with your left hand on your right shoulder.
esercizi.whatToDo: Perform the movement with calm breathing.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not force the movement if you feel a sharp pain in your shoulder.
esercizi.imageAlt: Touch the Opposite Shoulder

esercizi.nome: Lift the Hands from the Table Toward the Chest
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the front part of the arm from a comfortable and safe position, making it excellent for starting gymnastics.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright at a table and place your forearms and open hands on the table, with your palms facing upward. From this position, bend your elbows and lift your hands off the table, bringing them toward your chest. Your elbows must remain resting on the table. Count to 3, then place your hands back on the table.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your fingers relaxed and open.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your elbows off the table while raising your hands.
esercizi.imageAlt: Lift the Hands from the Table Toward the Chest

esercizi.nome: Squeeze a Pillow Between the Hands and Chest
esercizi.description: This movement uses a soft household object to strengthen the arm muscles used to hug grandchildren or hold cherished objects close.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on the chair. Take a small, soft cushion and hold it in front of your chest, squeezing it between the palms of your hands. Bend your arms forward and press the cushion against your chest, tightening the muscles at the front of your arms. Hold the pressure for 5 seconds, then relax your grip.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your back well supported against the backrest of the chair.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not clench your teeth and continue breathing normally.
esercizi.imageAlt: Squeeze a Pillow Between the Hands and Chest

esercizi.nome: The Waiter Plate Exercise
esercizi.description: This exercise helps build strength and endurance in the front part of the arm, which is useful for clearing the table or moving objects without dropping them.
esercizi.howToDo: You can sit or stand. Bend your right elbow to 90 degrees, keeping it close to your side, with your hand open and facing upward, as if you were a waiter holding a plate. Stay still in this position for 5 seconds, keeping the arm muscle active. Repeat with your left arm.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the palm of your hand flat and facing the ceiling.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move your elbow away from your side and do not lift your shoulder.
esercizi.imageAlt: The Waiter Plate Exercise

esercizi.nome: Press the Hands Down on the Table
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the muscles at the back of the arm using a safe surface. It helps provide stability when leaning on a piece of furniture.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright in front of a table. Place the palms of your hands on the table, keeping your elbows bent and close to your body. Push your hands firmly downward against the table, as if you were trying to lift yourself from the chair using only your arms. Maintain a steady push for 5 seconds, then relax.
esercizi.whatToDo: Feel the muscles at the back of your arms tighten during the push.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not let your neck sink between your shoulders and continue breathing.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Hands Down on the Table

esercizi.nome: Extend the Arm Forward
esercizi.description: This movement trains the back part of the arm in the action of reaching out for an object or cleaning a vertical surface.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright or stand. Bring your right hand close to your right shoulder with your elbow bent. From this position, fully extend your arm forward at chest height, as if you were giving a slow push or cleaning a window. Tighten the muscle at the back of your arm for 2 seconds, then slowly return. Repeat 5 times, then switch arms.
esercizi.whatToDo: Perform the movement very slowly, both when extending the arm and when returning.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not swing your torso forward to help the arm movement.
esercizi.imageAlt: Extend the Arm Forward

esercizi.nome: Push the Arms Back Against the Wall
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise safely activates the back part of the arm while keeping the back fully protected.
esercizi.howToDo: Stand with your back against a flat wall. Keep your arms by your sides, with your elbows close to your body. Firmly push the back of your arms against the wall. Maintain this steady push for 5 seconds, then relax the pressure.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your feet slightly away from the wall to stay more stable.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not push with your head or back; the force should come only from your arms.
esercizi.imageAlt: Push the Arms Back Against the Wall

esercizi.nome: Extend the Arms Upward with a Pillow
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the muscles at the back of the arm. It simulates the movement used to put on a hat, pass a towel behind your head after a shower, or place a light object on a shelf.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair with a backrest. Hold a light cushion with both hands. Bring your hands above your head, bending your elbows backward, so the cushion lightly touches the back of your neck. From this position, push your arms upward until they are fully extended toward the ceiling. Count to 2 at the highest point, then slowly bend your elbows again behind your head.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your elbows as close to your ears as possible, rather than wide apart.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not push your head forward and do not arch your back.
esercizi.imageAlt: Extend the Arms Upward with a Pillow

esercizi.nome: Show the Palm and Back of the Hand
esercizi.description: This exercise trains rotation of the elbow and forearm. It reproduces the movement used to turn a key, use a screwdriver, or turn food in a pan.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Bend your elbows to 90 degrees and keep them close to your sides, with your hands extended forward, as if you were holding a tray. Rotate your hands so that your palms face the floor. Then rotate them in the opposite direction so that your palms face the ceiling. Repeat this change 10 times in a row, calmly.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your elbows firmly close to your sides; only the forearm should rotate.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move your arms away from your body and do not move your shoulders.
esercizi.imageAlt: Show the Palm and Back of the Hand

esercizi.nome: Press the Elbow Against the Other Hand
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise strengthens the muscles that stabilize the elbow, which is useful for providing stability when leaning on furniture or using a walking stick.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright. Bend your right arm in front of your chest at 90 degrees. Place the palm of your left hand under your right elbow. Push your right elbow downward and, at the same time, use your left hand like a wall to push upward and stop the movement. Hold the push for 5 seconds, then switch arms.
esercizi.whatToDo: Continue breathing normally during the push.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not let your neck sink between your shoulders and do not push so hard that you feel pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Elbow Against the Other Hand

esercizi.nome: Clench the Fists and Extend the Arms Downward
esercizi.description: This movement trains full and controlled elbow extension, which is useful for safely stretching the arms when putting on trousers or a skirt.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on the chair. Start with your elbows bent and your hands close to your armpits, gently making fists. From this position, slowly extend your arms downward, pushing your hands toward the floor as if you were trying to touch the chair legs. Straighten your elbows well, open your hands at the lowest point, count to 2, then slowly return to the starting position.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your shoulders relaxed and away from your ears.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make jerky or very fast movements, to avoid jolting the joint.
esercizi.imageAlt: Clench the Fists and Extend the Arms Downward

esercizi.nome: The Balance Exercise with Hands on the Table
esercizi.description: This exercise uses the table to strengthen the elbow muscles alternately, improving stability and coordination of the arms.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit in front of a table and place the palms of both hands on the surface, keeping your elbows bent. Push downward with your right hand, slightly straightening the elbow, as if you were trying to lower one side of a scale, while the left arm relaxes. Then do the opposite: push with the left hand and relax the right. Alternate this slow movement 5 times on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your feet firmly on the floor to help with balance.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lean your torso to the right or left; the push should come only from the elbows.
esercizi.imageAlt: The Balance Exercise with Hands on the Table

esercizi.nome: Seated Elbow Press into the Table
esercizi.description: This isometric exercise strengthens the muscles around the elbow and helps improve stability when leaning on a table or armrest.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright in front of a table. Bend your right elbow and place the outer side of your forearm on the table. Gently press your elbow and forearm downward against the table without moving the arm. Hold the pressure for 5 seconds, then relax. Repeat with the left arm.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your shoulder relaxed while pressing down.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lean your whole body onto the arm; the pressure should come from the elbow and forearm.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Elbow Press into the Table

esercizi.nome: Wring Out a Dry Towel
esercizi.description: This exercise greatly strengthens all the muscles of the forearm and hands, simulating the action of wringing out a cleaning cloth or a sponge.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Take a small face towel, roll it up, and hold it with both hands in front of your chest. Turn your right hand forward and your left hand backward, as if you were strongly wringing out the towel. Hold the maximum squeeze for 4 seconds, then change direction, with the right hand turning backward and the left hand forward.
esercizi.whatToDo: Squeeze firmly and make slow, controlled movements.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not raise your shoulders toward your ears and do not hold your breath.
esercizi.imageAlt: Wring Out a Dry Towel

esercizi.nome: Open and Close the Fingers
esercizi.description: This movement trains the endurance of the forearm muscles, which is useful for preventing your arms from getting tired when writing, cooking, or doing small manual tasks.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit comfortably. Bring your hands forward to chest height with your elbows bent. Open your hands, spreading all your fingers as wide as possible. Immediately after, close your hands into fists and squeeze firmly. Alternate opening and closing your hands continuously 10 times in a row.
esercizi.whatToDo: Feel the forearm muscles moving and tightening under the skin.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make movements that are too quick or jerky; focus on making a strong, tight fist.
esercizi.imageAlt: Open and Close the Fingers

esercizi.nome: Raise the Wrist Up and Down with Support
esercizi.description: This exercise safely strengthens the upper and lower parts of the forearm while protecting the wrist joint.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit at a table. Rest your right forearm on the table, leaving your hand over the edge, with your palm facing the floor. Lift your hand upward, pointing your fingers toward the ceiling, without lifting your arm from the table. Hold the position for 2 seconds, then let your hand drop downward. Repeat 5 times, then switch arms.
esercizi.whatToDo: If you want to make the exercise more challenging, you can hold a small empty water bottle in your hand.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your elbow or forearm off the table; only your wrist should move.
esercizi.imageAlt: Raise the Wrist Up and Down with Support

esercizi.nome: Wrap an Imaginary Thread
esercizi.description: This movement improves forearm coordination and agility, simulating the action of rolling up a thread, a rope, or a television cable.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright. Bring your fists in front of your chest. Start rotating your hands one over the other, as if you were winding wool into a ball. Rotate your hands forward for 5 seconds, then stop and rotate them backward for another 5 seconds.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your elbows close to your body to avoid tiring your shoulders.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move your neck or head while rotating your hands.
esercizi.imageAlt: Wrap an Imaginary Thread

esercizi.nome: Seated Palm Turn with Light Object
esercizi.description: This exercise trains forearm rotation and grip control, useful for turning a door handle, using cutlery, or opening a small container.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Hold a light object, such as a small empty bottle, in your right hand. Keep your elbow bent to 90 degrees and close to your side. Slowly turn your palm upward, then slowly turn it downward. Repeat 5 times, then switch hands.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your elbow close to your body throughout the movement.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move your shoulder or twist your torso to help the movement.
esercizi.imageAlt: Seated Palm Turn with Light Object

esercizi.nome: Push the Palm Back with the Other Hand
esercizi.description: This exercise helps stretch the muscles and tendons of the wrist, improving the ability to bend the hand back when leaning on a chair or table.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright on a chair. Stretch your right arm forward at chest height, with the palm of your hand facing forward and your fingers pointing toward the ceiling, as if you were saying “stop.” With your left hand, gently hold the fingers of your right hand and slowly pull them toward your body. Stop when you feel a gentle stretch under the wrist. Count to 5, then switch arms.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your arm straight, but without straining your shoulder.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not pull your fingers too forcefully and stop immediately if you feel pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Push the Palm Back with the Other Hand

esercizi.nome: Press the Backs of the Hands Together
esercizi.description: This movement provides the opposite stretch, releasing tension in the upper part of the wrist and hand.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright. This time, bring your hands together in front of your chest, placing the back of your right hand against the back of your left hand, with your fingers pointing downward. Gently press your hands against each other for 5 seconds, feeling a stretch around the wrist area.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your shoulders relaxed and away from your ears.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not make sudden movements and stop immediately if you feel pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Press the Backs of the Hands Together

esercizi.nome: Turn the Wrist Sideways
esercizi.description: This exercise trains the side movements of the wrist, which are essential for using a spoon properly, turning a ladle while cooking, or pouring water.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit at a table and rest your right forearm on the surface, keeping your hand flat with your palm facing down. Without lifting your arm from the table, move your hand to the right and then to the left, sliding it across the table as if you were waving to someone from far away. Repeat this movement 5 times, then switch arms.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move only your hand; your arm should remain completely still on the table.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift your fingers or palm off the table during the movement.
esercizi.imageAlt: Turn the Wrist Sideways

esercizi.nome: Make Circles in the Air with the Hands
esercizi.description: This movement helps lubricate the wrist joint, reducing the stiffness that older adults often feel in the morning when they first wake up.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit comfortably. Bring your hands forward with your elbows bent and close to your sides. Close your hands into very light fists. Start rotating your wrists, drawing circles in the air with your hands. Make 5 circles clockwise, toward the right, and then 5 circles counterclockwise, toward the left.
esercizi.whatToDo: Rotate your hands very slowly, trying to make each circle as large as possible.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not move your arms or elbows; the movement should come only from your wrists.
esercizi.imageAlt: Make Circles in the Air with the Hands

esercizi.nome: Touch the Fingertips with the Thumb
esercizi.description: This exercise trains finger precision and agility, which are essential for picking up small objects from a table, such as pills, coins, or needles.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit comfortably with your hands resting on your thighs. Lift your right hand with your palm open. Bring your thumb to touch the tip of your index finger, then the tip of your middle finger, ring finger, and finally your little finger. Once you reach the little finger, go back in the opposite direction. Repeat 3 times with each hand.
esercizi.whatToDo: Gently press one fingertip against the other when they touch.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not rush; the important thing is to touch the fingertips accurately and precisely.
esercizi.imageAlt: Touch the Fingertips with the Thumb

esercizi.nome: Finger Walk on the Table
esercizi.description: This movement strengthens and stretches the finger muscles, imitating the action of typing on a phone keyboard or on a remote control.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit upright in front of a table. Place your right hand open on the table, keeping the palm lifted so that only the fingertips touch the surface, as if your hand were a spider. Using only your fingertips, make your hand “walk” forward along the table and then back again. Repeat 5 times with each hand.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep your wrist still and relaxed; move only your fingers.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not press the palm of your hand against the table while moving.
esercizi.imageAlt: Finger Walk on the Table

esercizi.nome: Spread the Fingers Against an Elastic Band
esercizi.description: This exercise helps strengthen the muscles that open the hand, which is very useful for counteracting the tendency of older adults’ fingers to remain bent or stiff.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit comfortably. Bring the fingertips of your right hand together. Place a regular kitchen elastic band around the joined fingers, just below the fingernails. From this position, spread your fingers outward, pushing against the resistance of the elastic band. Hold the open position for 3 seconds, then slowly close your fingers again. Repeat 5 times on each side.
esercizi.whatToDo: Perform the movement very slowly, both when opening and closing your fingers.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not use an elastic band that is too tight, as it may hurt or cut the skin.
esercizi.imageAlt: Spread the Fingers Against an Elastic Band

esercizi.nome: Pick Up and Crumple a Sheet of Paper
esercizi.description: This exercise greatly strengthens the whole hand and trains the action of grasping and holding objects without letting them slip away.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit at a table. Place an old sheet of newspaper or a sheet of kitchen paper flat on the table in front of you. Place your open right hand on top of it. Using only the fingers of that hand, start pulling the paper toward the centre of your palm until you turn the sheet into a tight ball. Do the same with your left hand.
esercizi.whatToDo: Squeeze the ball firmly at the end, using the full strength of your hand.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not help yourself with the other hand; one hand must do all the work on its own.
esercizi.imageAlt: Pick Up and Crumple a Sheet of Paper

esercizi.nome: Table Finger Lifts
esercizi.description: This exercise improves finger control and strengthens the small muscles of the hand, useful for typing, buttoning clothes, and handling small objects.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit at a table and place your right hand flat on the surface, with your palm and fingers resting down. Keeping the palm still, slowly lift only your index finger, then lower it. Continue with the middle finger, ring finger, and little finger. Repeat 3 times, then switch hands.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move one finger at a time and keep the rest of the hand relaxed.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not lift the whole hand from the table.
esercizi.imageAlt: Table Finger Lifts

esercizi.nome: Finger Spread on the Table
esercizi.description: This exercise strengthens the muscles that open the fingers and helps maintain hand mobility during daily tasks such as grasping a glass or opening the hand to lean on a surface.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit at a table and place your right hand flat on the surface. Keeping your palm down, slowly spread your fingers apart as much as possible, then slowly bring them back together. Repeat 5 times, then switch hands.
esercizi.whatToDo: Keep the movement slow and controlled.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not force the fingers apart if you feel pain.
esercizi.imageAlt: Finger Spread on the Table

esercizi.nome: Thumb Slide Across the Palm
esercizi.description: This exercise improves thumb mobility, which is essential for grasping objects, opening containers, and holding cutlery.
esercizi.howToDo: Sit comfortably with your hands resting on your thighs. Open your right hand with the palm facing upward. Slowly slide your thumb across the palm toward the base of the little finger, then return to the open-hand position. Repeat 5 times, then switch hands.
esercizi.whatToDo: Move the thumb slowly and try to reach across the palm without forcing.
esercizi.whatNotToDo: Do not bend the wrist to help the thumb reach farther.
esercizi.imageAlt: Thumb Slide Across the Palm
